content.full_text:
October 30, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Re: Committees of Less Than A Quorum

Gentlemen:

I request an advice letter as to whether I, as a member of the
Board of Directors of the Cathedral City Redevelopment Agency, may
participate in committee meetings of the Redevelopment Agency
without violating the Ralph M. Brown Act, if the meetings are of
less than a quorum of the Board, and are closed to the public.

The City Attorney of Cathedral City, who also serves as Agency
Counsel for the Redevelopment Agency, has advised me that a
committee consisting of less than a quorum of the members of the
Board may properly meet with proponents of a possible development
while they outline their plans, and get some input from council
members, with no commitments made, unless the committee wants to
make a formal recommendation to the entire board. He says that such
committee meetings may be held in closed session. He refers me to
the provisions of Government Code Section 54952.3, fourth
paragraph, which provides: "'Legislative body' as defined in this
section does not include a committee composed solely of members of
the governing body of a local agency which are less than a quorum
of such governing body."

The Redevelopment Agency's established committees are outlined in
Resolution No. R-142, as amended by Resolution No. R-144, a copy
of each of which is attached for your information.

One council member informs me that such meetings are "secret
meetings" and that my participation in them may be unlawful. I
do not want to participate in any illegal meeting, but I do
recognize that committee meetings can serve a valuable purpose. I
will participate only if the meetings are legal.

68-625 PEREZ ROAD • P.O. BOX 5001 • CATHEDRAL CITY, CA 92235-5001 • 619 / 324-8388 • FAX: 619 / 324-4816

Fair Political Practices Commission
October 30, 1990
Page 2


Your issuing an advice letter to me concerning this matter will be
greatly appreciated.

Very truly yours,

[illegible signature]
Carol Engelhardt
City Councilwoman/RDA Board Member

sections.question: whether I, as a member of the Board of Directors of the Cathedral City Redevelopment Agency, may participate in committee meetings of the Redevelopment Agency without violating the Ralph M. Brown Act, if the meetings are of less than a quorum of the Board, and are closed to the public.
sections.question_synthetic: Can a Board member of the Cathedral City Redevelopment Agency legally participate in closed committee meetings composed of less than a quorum of the Board without violating the Ralph M. Brown Act?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. No verbatim QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are marked with headers. The question and request are embedded in the body of the letter. Document is dated October 30, 1990 and references specific resolutions and Government Code provisions.

embedding.summary: A Cathedral City Redevelopment Agency Board member requests an FPPC advice letter regarding the legality of participating in closed committee meetings of less than a quorum, citing Government Code section 54952.3 and guidance from the City Attorney, while noting concerns raised by another council member about whether such meetings constitute illegal secret meetings.
